items.blurb: A tiny cat drifting off with balloons, ready to float across your envelopes. Ink it, stamp it, smile every single time.
One mid-leap kitty to bounce across journal pages and snail mail. Small stamp, maximum spring.
A little feline announcer for anything worth announcing. Stamp it on notes, memos and letters that deserve a jingle.
A kitty mid-sip of milk, carved into wood and rubber. The gentlest way to sign off a letter or planner page.
This cat is on the phone and the call is adorable. Stamp your reminders and letters with a ring of charm.
Office admin, but make it kitten. A wee cat guards your enclosed papers with full bureaucratic cuteness.
A congratulatory cat for wins big and small. Press it onto cards whenever someone deserves confetti.
Top-secret correspondence, approved by cat. Mark your most confidential notes with a whiskered classified seal.
Close every envelope with a feline flourish. This little sealed-by-cat stamp makes even bills look lovable.
Approval never looked this fluffy. One press and your to-do list gets an official kitty OK.
Cats plus coffee, the two great comforts, in one wooden stamp. Perfect for journals kept in cafe corners.
A cozy cat-and-coffee-cup motif to warm up letters and planner spreads. Brew, stamp, repeat.
A cat calmly slicing bread, immortalised as a pin. Tote bags and denim jackets have never been hungrier.
A kitty with its own coffee cup, pinned wherever you need a caffeine companion. Tiny, tidy, terribly charming.
A cat on two wheels, breezing across your lapel. The commute upgrade nobody knew they needed.
A curled-up napping cat for your collar or bag. Wear it as a promise of the nap you deserve later.
A cat snoozing on a warm retro TV, shrunk to pin size. Peak vintage-cat behaviour, pinned forever.
A hopeful kitty asking the eternal question at dinnertime. Pin it on and let the begging eyes do the talking.
A cat delivering the post with great importance. For pen pals, letter lovers and anyone who still checks the mailbox.
A determined cat reaching for the candy jar, caught in the act. Sweet tooth, meet soft paw.
A cat pressing laundry with total focus. Domestic chores rendered impossibly endearing, one pin at a time.
Cats doing their accounts on an abacus, as one does. The most studious little brooch in your collection.
A musical kitty mid-performance, pinned to your bag like a backstage pass. Encore guaranteed.
A cat spinning records like a whiskered DJ. Clip it on and let your outfit drop the needle.
A tucked-in kitty ready for lights out. The bedtime pin for people whose love language is naps.
A cat peeking out of a hat, equal parts magic trick and mischief. Pin-sized whimsy for coats and pouches.
A cat and its duck friend, an unlikely duo you get to wear. Friendship goals in brooch form.
A cat making a swift, guilty exit. For anyone who has ever ghosted a party early, this pin understands.
A diligent cat clocking in at its tiny desk. The perfect co-worker: silent, fluffy, always on your lapel.
A kitty blowing soap bubbles, frozen in a dreamy pop. Softest little flourish for a canvas tote.
A shelf full of cats, neatly stacked like paperbacks. Librarian-core meets kawaii in one pin.
A cat hunting for a four-leaf clover on your behalf. Wear a little luck wherever you wander.
A furry safecracker mid-heist, pinned to your jacket. The cutest crime you will ever accessorise.
A cat pausing for a polite sip of water. Hydration icon, honestly. Sweet on bags, blazers and beanies.
A cat wielding a brush with scholarly grace. For stationery lovers who take their ink seriously-ish.
A thrifty kitty guarding its piggy bank. Pin it on as a gentle reminder of your savings goals.
A cat on a rotary phone, taking a very important call. Retro telecom charm for your favourite jacket.
A blissed-out cat lost in its favourite song. Clip on this tiny listener and vibe together.
Two cats locked in a strategic game of Go. Deeply serious, deeply adorable, deeply pinnable.
A cat posing among kokeshi dolls, a little slice of Japan for your lapel. Folk-craft charm, feline edition.
A cat toasting under a kotatsu, the coziest place on earth. Winter comfort, distilled into one pin.
A kitty happily munching an onigiri. Snack-time serenity you can wear to lunch every day.
Watercolour animals and a wandering fairy unspool across this clear PET tape. A whole storybook for your journal margins.
A wide watercolour strip of cats running their own cafe. Tear off a scene and your planner opens for business.
Seconds, please. This washi roll serves helping after helping of matching motifs so your spreads never go hungry.
An illustrated nudge to make today an adventure, painted in Taiwan and ready for your wall, desk or a lucky mailbox.
Fizzy die-cut clear tape with a gift-wrapped sparkle. Stick it on jars, journals and parcels for instant effervescence.
A handmade canvas pouch with leather-tipped corners that only get lovelier with wear. Small enough to tuck, sturdy enough to trust.
The roomier sibling of a cult-favourite canvas pouch, hand-finished in Okayama. Carries pens, cables and your whole aesthetic.
A suede-lined leather sleeve that treats two fountain pens like royalty. Heirloom energy for your everyday carry.
Leather and brass join forces to keep two pens clipped to your notebook. Handsome now, gorgeous once the patina sets in.
A solid brass badge that quietly declares your love of handmade things, ageing into its own patina as you wear it.
A heart-stamped brass charm for notebook straps and bag pulls. It mellows with time, just like a good story.
Artist eric's travel-blessing stamp, straight from her Japan exhibition. Press a bon voyage onto every journal spread.
A little wooden stamp of good times, by illustrator eric. Ink it whenever the day earns a keepsake.
eric's cheerful stamp comes with a tiny square made for dates. Log your happiest days with one satisfying press.
Cozy illustrated girls campaigning for more snail mail, in sticker form. Your envelopes are about to get chatty.
A gentle illustrated plea to send more mail, which is itself mail. Poetic. Frame it or post it onward.
Soft-girl illustrations with a bakery-case of sweets, sheet after sticky sheet. Dessert for your planner, zero crumbs.
A dreamy dessert-laden postcard by illustrator 252%. Sweet enough to mail, sweeter pinned above your desk.
A picnic of cozy illustrated girls, packed onto one sticker sheet. Spread the blanket across any journal page.
A sunny illustrated picnic scene, cat included, on a made-in-Japan postcard. Alfresco charm for wall or mailbox.
Round sticky notes that change colour story every few sheets. Twelve looks, one pad, endless excuses to leave notes.
Checkered sticky notes in a dozen rotating patterns. Your reminders just picked up a picnic-blanket wardrobe.
Tidy grid sticky notes that shuffle through twelve designs as you go. For lists that like a little structure.
Fizzy bath-bomb rounds in twelve swirling designs. The most relaxing to-do list format ever invented.
An accordion of 28 board-game-inspired washi strips that tear by hand. Unfold it and let your planner roll the dice.
Pocket washi strips plus silver-foil mini stickers in soft watercolour washes. Layer them into tiny planner masterpieces.
Gold-foiled message stickers and washi strips in one wallet-sized kit. Little words that catch the light and the eye.
Planets and foil shimmer, folded into a pocket-sized sticker kit. Give your journal its own little galaxy.
Pre-cut washi strips in a card-slim folder that lives in your planner pocket. Collage anywhere, scissors optional.
A grid notebook wrapped in Ayumi Miyahara florals, with eight hidden pockets for sticker stashes. Undated, unhurried, lovely.
Dandelions dusted with gold foil drift down this washi roll. Spring wishes for the edges of every page.
Shiba inu and wagashi share a gold-foiled washi reel. Snacks and floof, the two pillars of a good planner.
Six mood stamps in one clicky push-button gadget. Log your feelings with a press, then colour them in.
Pochacco goes holographic in this limited mizutama collab sticker. Shiny, scarce and extremely deserving of prime laptop real estate.
Deep-cut Sanrio alert: Goropikadon in shimmering hologram, drawn by mizutama. A collector's flex in sticker form.
One hundred sheets of Snoopy letter paper, double-sided and tear-out ready. Your pen pals are about to be spoiled.
A hundred sheets of seasonal botanicals for letters, wrapping and collage. A whole year of moods in one pad.
Liberty-style florals across one hundred removable sheets. Letters, gift wrap and journal pages, all suddenly in bloom.
